content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all three pending amendments for planned-elsewhere mapping durability and test coverage. Amendment 1: Added 4 new tests in service.rs covering journal rebuild when NDJSON is missing, identity-based collapse where verified supersedes unverified, graceful skipping of journal events with missing metadata fields, and sidecar-divergence resolution where a journal-only mapping (simulating NDJSON write failure) is still visible on reload. Fixed the pre-existing persistence_survives_reload test in planned_elsewhere.rs to not depend on HashMap iteration order. Amendment 2: Rewrote load_planned_elsewhere_mappings to always rebuild from the authoritative journal via rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal, which collapses rows by identity key (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) keeping the latest journal event. This ensures that a verified mapping (mapped_bead_verified=true) appended after the original unverified record supersedes it, preventing duplicate re-verification and duplicate br comments on replay. Amendment 3: The same journal-first rewrite solves the sidecar divergence problem: since load_planned_elsewhere_mappings now always reads from the journal (not the NDJSON sidecar), a mapping that was successfully journaled but whose NDJSON append failed is always visible. The NDJSON sidecar is retained as a write-through projection but is no longer consulted during reads.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendments 2+3: Rewrote load_planned_elsewhere_mappings to always rebuild from journal via rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal, removing the sidecar-first read path. rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal now uses a HashMap keyed by (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) so later journal events supersede earlier ones, collapsing verified records over unverified ones.
2. [Completed] Amendment 1: Added planned_elsewhere_rebuild_from_journal_when_ndjson_missing test — creates a mapping, deletes the NDJSON sidecar, verifies load recovers all fields from journal.
3. [Completed] Amendment 1: Added planned_elsewhere_collapse_verified_supersedes_unverified test — writes unverified then verified mapping for same identity, verifies load returns only 1 collapsed verified record.
4. [Completed] Amendment 1: Added planned_elsewhere_journal_events_with_missing_metadata_skipped test — appends malformed events (missing mapped_to_bead_id, no metadata), verifies they are gracefully skipped.
5. [Completed] Amendment 1: Added planned_elsewhere_sidecar_divergence_resolved_by_journal test — writes one mapping normally, then writes a second mapping only to journal (simulating NDJSON failure), verifies both are visible on load.
6. [Completed] Fixed persistence_survives_reload test in planned_elsewhere.rs to use sorted comparison instead of index-based assertions, since HashMap-based rebuild does not guarantee insertion order.
7. [Completed] Run cargo test (762 passed: 682 lib + 174 integration + 1 unit, 0 failed), cargo fmt --check (clean), cargo clippy (no new warnings).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 762 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (682 lib up from 678, 174 integration, 1 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo test --lib service::tests::planned_elsewhere: 4 new tests pass (rebuild_from_journal_when_ndjson_missing, collapse_verified_supersedes_unverified, journal_events_with_missing_metadata_skipped, sidecar_divergence_resolved_by_journal)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file; all 19 warnings are pre-existing
- Amendment 2 verified: load_planned_elsewhere_mappings now always rebuilds from journal with identity-based collapse; HashMap keyed by (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) ensures latest event wins, so verified=true supersedes verified=false
- Amendment 3 verified: journal-first read means NDJSON sidecar divergence cannot cause mappings to disappear; planned_elsewhere_sidecar_divergence_resolved_by_journal test confirms a journal-only mapping is visible even when NDJSON has fewer rows
- Amendment 1 verified: 4 new tests cover rebuild from journal, identity collapse, malformed event skipping, and sidecar divergence; persistence_survives_reload test updated for order-independent assertion
- Files modified: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs (journal-first loader + 4 tests), src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs (order-independent test assertion)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change
- One flaky integration test (checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_creates_finds_and_resets_checkpoint_commits) occasionally fails due to git timing; unrelated to this change
- The journal-first read path scans the full journal on every load, which could be slow for very large journals; acceptable because planned-elsewhere events are rare and milestones have bounded lifetimes
- The _mapping_store parameter in load_planned_elsewhere_mappings is now unused (prefixed with underscore); the NDJSON sidecar is still written during record_planned_elsewhere_mapping for forward compatibility but is not read during loads
